what_it_does: Records metadata for a single image attachment in the design-assets channel, linking it to a target image asset and noting when it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
Fields present: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
The filename matches the id value 'att-2bd44ed2'.
standouts: The target URI contains spaces and parentheses ('image (49)'), which is unusual for a reference identifier.
The record is extremely minimal, containing only five metadata fields.
conventions: Filename is derived from the record id.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset.
Uses a custom 'image://' URI scheme to locate the target asset.
concerns: The target string contains spaces and parentheses, which may cause parsing or linking fragility.
No descriptive title, dimensions, MIME type, or author is recorded.
The asset name 'image (49)' is generic and gives no semantic hint about the image content.
notes: A bare ledger entry that attaches an image asset to the design-assets channel via a custom image:// URI.
questions: What does the '(49)' suffix in the target represent?
Is the image:// URI resolvable by the consuming system?
Why is there no title, alt text, or file-size metadata?
intention_signals: It appears intended to create a timestamped, typed record that a specific image asset was added to the design-assets channel.
novelty: The use of a custom image:// URI with a channel path and a numbered, space-containing asset name.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed reference that links a channel attachment identifier to a specific image asset location.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter attachment ledger record for the design-assets channel that references the image asset 'image://design-assets/icons/image (49)' and records an added timestamp of 2026-06-22T12:26:26.319172+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to log that an image attachment was added to the design-assets channel and to point to its target asset location.
